skill.name: resume-bullet-extraction
skill.description: Auto-invoke after task completion to generate powerful resume bullet points from completed work.
skill.summary: Auto-invoke after task completion to generate powerful resume bullet points from completed work.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown templates and career-writing prose. No executable code, network calls, filesystem automation, secret access, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Templates
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and resume bullet examples, not executable instructions or scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain Markdown examples and file path examples only. There is no command interpreter, script file, or instruction to execute these blocks.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Prose
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words inside career guidance text, not dangerous functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose headings, resume vocabulary, and checklist items at these lines. No cryptographic API, reconnaissance command, or network tool appears in the file.

content.user_title: Turn Completed Work Into Resume Bullets
content.value_statement: Technical work is often hard to translate into clear resume impact. This skill turns completed projects into concise bullets with action verbs, technologies, and measurable results.
actual_capabilities: Structures achievements with action verb, work performed, technical context, and impact.
Suggests stronger verbs for building, improving, problem solving, and collaboration work.
Guides users to quantify performance, reliability, business, and scale outcomes.
Provides templates for features, optimizations, bug fixes, architecture, and refactoring work.
Connects resume bullets to STAR story files for later extraction.
limitations: It does not verify metrics against production data or analytics systems.
It cannot guarantee recruiter or employer response to a resume.
It focuses on technical accomplishments and may need adaptation for nontechnical roles.
It provides writing guidance, not legal, immigration, or hiring advice.

use_cases.title: Convert Finished Features Into Resume Evidence
use_cases.description: Create clear bullets after completing a feature, using the technology stack and user impact from the work.

use_cases.title: Improve Project Portfolio Descriptions
use_cases.description: Rewrite side project descriptions so they show ownership, technical choices, and measurable outcomes.

use_cases.title: Prepare Interview Story Material
use_cases.description: Capture concise achievement statements that can later support STAR interview stories.

prompt_templates.title: Create One Bullet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn my completed task into one resume bullet. Task: [describe the task]. Technologies: [list technologies]. Impact: [describe result].

prompt_templates.title: Generate Several Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft three resume bullet options for this project. Include different action verbs and keep each option to one sentence.

prompt_templates.title: Add Measurable Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to strengthen this resume bullet with measurable impact. Ask up to three questions if the metric is unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Refine For Senior Impact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to rewrite these bullets for a senior software engineering resume. Emphasize architecture, scale, reliability, and business value.

output_examples.input: Built a dashboard with React and charting tools for support teams.
output_examples.output: Built a React analytics dashboard that gave support teams real-time visibility into customer issues and reduced manual status checks.

output_examples.input: Fixed slow API calls by adding indexes and changing queries.
output_examples.output: Optimized database queries through index analysis and query restructuring, reducing average API response time and improving user workflows.

output_examples.input: Moved authentication from sessions to tokens.
output_examples.output: Refactored authentication from session storage to token-based access, reducing server memory usage and improving scalability.

best_practices: Provide the completed task, technologies used, and the user or business outcome.
Use measured results when available, and label estimates as estimates.
Keep each bullet to one sentence with a strong action verb.
anti_patterns: Do not invent metrics or business impact that you cannot explain.
Do not use passive phrases such as responsible for or helped with.
Do not overload bullets with internal jargon that recruiters cannot understand.

faq.question: Does this skill write a full resume?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on extracting strong resume bullets from completed technical work.

faq.question: Can it help when I do not have exact metrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts for reasonable impact signals, but estimates should be honest and clearly defensible.

faq.question: Is this only for software engineers?
faq.answer: It is strongest for technical work, but the formula can help with product, data, and operations achievements.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it store my resume bullets automatically?
faq.answer: The skill describes compiling bullets into STAR story files, but actual storage depends on the host tool and workspace.

faq.question: Will it guarantee that my resume passes screening?
faq.answer: No. It improves clarity and impact, but screening depends on role fit, market conditions, and employer criteria.
